channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Pro-union Trump nominee faces Senate grilling as at least one Republican vows to oppose her
item.description: Lori Chavez-DeRemer will go before the Senate HELP committee on Wednesday for a confirmation hearing. Sen. Rand Paul, who has already said he will vote "no," is on the committee.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk’s budget-slashing hits political reality of suffering Americans
item.description: While almost everyone agrees that the federal government has become this monstrous leviathan that needs to be curtailed, they don't all agree with the Trump-Musk approach to it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Justice Department and New York Mayor Adams face judge's scrutiny in bid to dismiss criminal charges
item.description: Justice Department lawyers and New York City Mayor Eric Adams are set to face a federal judge who's signaling that he's unlikely to rubber stamp their request to drop corruption charges weeks before an April trial.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister And Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump administration halts support for representing unaccompanied children in immigration court
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration on Tuesday stopped support for legal representation in immigration court for children who enter the United States alone, a setback for those fighting deportation who can't afford a lawyer.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOGE is getting lists of the military's probationary workers from the Pentagon
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Department of Government Efficiency staffers were at the Pentagon on Tuesday and receiving lists of the military’s probationary employees, U.S. officials said.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Scott Stringer: I hope Hochul suspends NYC Mayor Eric Adams
item.description: Stringer, who hopes to unseat Adams in June's Democratic primary for mayor, believes "we have to resolve this, one way or another.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Trump signs executive order asking for recommendations on lowering IVF costs
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order directing federal agencies to find ways to reduce the high cost of in vitro fertilization (IVF) treatment.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Border arrests plummet 39% in January in an early gauge of Trump's immigration policies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Arrests for illegal border crossings from Mexico plummeted 39% in January from a month earlier, authorities said Tuesday, an early gauge of President Donald Trump's immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge refuses to block Musk, DOGE from 7 federal agencies
item.description: U.S. District Judge Tanya Chutkan on Tuesday refused the request of 14 Democratic state attorneys general to immediately impose wide-ranging restrictions on Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE).
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Supervisor in DC federal prosecutors' office told to resign after dispute over investigation
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A top supervisor in the federal prosecutors' office in Washington said she was forced to resign following a dispute with her boss over a directive that she scrutinize the awarding of a government contract during the Biden administration, according to a letter reviewed by The Associ
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's Labor Department pick has union support. Worker advocates wonder how much power she'd have
item.description: President Donald Trump’s nominee to lead the Department of Labor has been hailed by union heads as pro-labor.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Bussewitz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Retail recreational marijuana, take two. Cannabis management leader speaks to rollout
item.description: On Tuesday, the Office of Cannabis Management started accepting applications for licenses to grow and sell marijuana — for a second time. Eric Taubel, interim director, joined Morning Edition host Cathy Wurzer Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Trump signs order to study how to expand IVF and calls for 'radical transparency' from government
item.description: President Donald Trump has signed an executive order to study how to expand access to in vitro fertilization and make it more affordable.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Leonard Peltier leaves prison after Biden commuted his sentence in the killing of two FBI agents
item.description: Indigenous activist Leonard Peltier has been released from a high-security Florida prison. Peltier was freed Tuesday morning, nearly a month after then-President Joe Biden commuted his life sentence in the 1975 killings of two FBI agents.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Black-owned businesses say DEI initiatives fell short
item.description: Trump’s rollback of DEI initiatives leaves some Black-owned business leaders unfazed. Biden’s DEI policies delivered some gains — but not enough to overcome structural barriers. ‘Good old boy’ network and other barriers shut minority-owned firms out of federal government contracts, some firms say.
item.dc:creator: MPR News

item.title: U.S. and Russia discuss ending Ukraine war, without Kyiv
item.description: The talks build on last week’s phone call between President Trump and Russian leader Vladimir Putin during which the two leaders agreed to work together toward a negotiated end to the war.
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes, MPR News

item.title: RFK Jr. rescinds Indian Health Service layoffs
item.description: The last-minute decision to rescind the layoff order for IHS came as health care workers waited for bad news.
item.dc:creator: MPR News

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu signals he's moving ahead with Trump's idea to transfer Palestinians from Gaza
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Sunday signaled that he was moving ahead with U.S. President Donald Trump's proposal to transfer the Palestinian population out of Gaza, calling it “the only viable plan to enable a different future" for the region.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Activist Curtis Sliwa joins race for NYC mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The New York City mayoral race just got even more crowded. The founder of the Guardian Angels nonprofit, Curtis Sliwa, has officially filed paperwork to run for the city's top job. He also ran for mayor back in 2021 and lost to the current Mayor Eric Adams.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Florida passes immigration bill, repealing in-state tuition for undocumented students
item.description: The third and final day of the special session concluded with the passage of an immigration bill, one that sparked significant debate among lawmakers in both the House and Senate.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
